key_points: Holozoic nutrition involves the ingestion, digestion, absorption, and assimilation of solid organic matter (e.g., Amoeba, Humans).
The human digestive system uses biological catalysts called enzymes (like salivary amylase, pepsin, and trypsin) to break down food.
Villi are finger-like projections in the small intestine that increase surface area for maximum absorption of digested food.
Aerobic respiration occurs in the presence of oxygen (releasing more energy), while anaerobic respiration occurs in its absence.
In plants, Xylem transports water and minerals from roots to leaves, while Phloem translocates food (sucrose) from leaves to all parts.
examples: Amoeba Nutrition: Uses pseudopodia to engulf food particles into a food vacuole.
Stomatal Movement: Guard cells swell (opening the pore) or shrink (closing the pore) based on water flow.
Dental Caries: Gradual softening of enamel and dentine due to acids produced by bacteria from sugar.
Anaerobic Respiration in Yeast: Produces Ethanol and $CO_2$ (Fermentation).
Anaerobic Respiration in Muscle Cells: Produces Lactic Acid during vigorous exercise, causing cramps.
formulas: Breakdown of Glucose (Pathway 1): $	ext{Glucose} \xrightarrow{	ext{Cytoplasm}} 	ext{Pyruvate} \xrightarrow{	ext{Absence of } O_2} 	ext{Ethanol} + CO_2 + 	ext{Energy}$
Breakdown of Glucose (Pathway 2): $	ext{Glucose} \xrightarrow{	ext{Cytoplasm}} 	ext{Pyruvate} \xrightarrow{	ext{Lack of } O_2} 	ext{Lactic Acid} + 	ext{Energy}$
Transpiration: $	ext{Loss of water vapor} ightarrow 	ext{Suction pull} ightarrow 	ext{Ascent of Sap}$
exam_tips: Remember that the small intestine is the site of complete digestion of carbohydrates, proteins, and fats.
Note that bile juice (from the liver) does not contain enzymes but is essential for the emulsification of fats.
Understand that the length of the small intestine varies; herbivores have longer ones to digest cellulose, while carnivores have shorter ones.
Distinguish between Alveoli (site of gas exchange in lungs) and Nephrons (filtration units in kidneys).
